steps.title: Match reception to earnings
steps.content: The journalist claims that a quality signal such as Oscar recognition can boost long-term success despite a modest opening weekend. The needed evidence should compare an Oscar-recognized film with a smaller opening weekend but higher lifetime earnings than a nonrecognized film.

steps.title: Remove weaker film comparisons
steps.content: A contradicts the claim because the nonrecognized film had higher lifetime earnings. C compares two Oscar-recognized films, so it does not isolate the effect of recognition. D compares two films that were not recognized by the Oscars. The remaining comparison fits the claim. The answer is B.
